methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 2 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 21 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: QuantumScape
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $5B+

private.name: LanzaTech
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Public), post-money $800M+

data.narrative:
Brimstone is a public-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector founded by Cody Finke and having raised $100M+. The valuation universe consists of 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers, including QuantumScape, LanzaTech, TAE Technologies, Redwood Materials, and Helion. The Climate & Energy sector contains 21 same-sector peers total.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable in this instance. The narrow peer set—filtered by stage, adjacent stage, and similar valuation tier—yielded fewer than 3 datapoints across all filters. Specifically, the analysis identified 2 same-stage comparables, 0 adjacent-stage peers, and 0 similar-tier peers, falling short of the minimum 3-company threshold required for reliable median-based triangulation. A sector-wide median across all 21 Climate & Energy peers was rejected as uninformative, given the substantial heterogeneity in stage, capital intensity, and commercialization maturity typical of the sector.

Without sufficient narrow peer density to support triangulation, no valuation range can be derived from the available data. The key uncertainty is the scarcity of directly comparable transactions or public filings at Brimstone's stage and technology specificity within Climate & Energy. Future valuation work would benefit from either expanded transaction data, public market signals from the single listed peer (LNZA), or discounted cash flow modeling anchored to technology development milestones and addressable market estimates.
